Hiring tool

Video Editor Job Brief Builder

Turn a vague editor request into a brief that tells editors what they need to know before they apply, quote, or recommend someone better suited.

This tool supports hiring-intent SEO and gives community posts a useful destination before asking teams to submit a full hiring brief.

Build

Fill the fields editors use to judge fit

0% Needs detail
  • Role type
  • Content format
  • Deliverables
  • Footage volume
  • Budget
  • Timeline
  • Software
  • Revision process
  • References

Generated brief

Add the details on the left and this preview will turn them into a cleaner editor brief.

See template

The builder saves only to this browser until you choose to submit the hiring form.

Why this works better than a vague post

Editors need enough context to decide whether their portfolio, speed, software, and rate fit the work. A clear brief reduces mismatched applications and makes stronger editors more likely to reply.

What to do after the score improves

Use the generated brief as a starting point, then submit the hiring request. The post-job form routes budget, workflow, references, and review details into the hiring queue.

Answers

Questions editors and hiring teams ask first

What should a video editor job brief include?

A useful brief should include content format, deliverables, source footage volume, budget, timeline, software, review process, references, and what a great editor should already understand.

Can I use this for freelance and remote editor jobs?

Yes. The builder works for freelance, remote, YouTube, short-form, podcast, agency, brand, assistant editor, and full-time editing roles.

Does the builder submit my job automatically?

No. It creates a local draft in your browser. You choose whether to continue to the post-job form and submit the hiring request.
